rationale: The US-Iran exchange of strikes and Hormuz closure continues for a second day — this is a continuation of the known situation with no qualitative escalation, so the baseline decays slightly, but the Hormuz closure still poses indirect risk to European energy prices relevant to Poland.

top_events.title: US-Iran strikes continue; Hormuz closed, Iran claims 18 targets hit
top_events.summary: For the second consecutive day, the US and Iran are exchanging strikes across the Middle East. Iran claims it hit 18 US targets and confirms the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. A separate report confirms a ship was hit off Oman with 3 dead. This is a continuation of the known conflict — no qualitative new escalation (e.g. no NATO involvement, no nuclear use), but the prolonged Hormuz closure keeps indirect pressure on global oil supply and energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: Trump: 'I love the inflation' as US prices hit 3-year high
top_events.summary: US inflation is rising at its fastest rate in three years, and Trump expressed indifference. Higher US inflation can strengthen the dollar, tighten global financial conditions, and indirectly affect Polish import costs and NBP monetary policy decisions — a mild but real indirect economic signal for Poland.

top_events.title: UK Defence Secretary Healey resigns over defence spending
top_events.summary: Britain's Defence Secretary John Healey has resigned over defence spending disputes. This is a continuation of the known situation — no new qualitative development. It remains a background concern for NATO cohesion, which indirectly affects Poland's security umbrella.
